Description.Name: Albatross
Description.Details: The Albatross was originally designed by the Free Worlds League in 3053 with an eye toward a possible future conflict with the then advancing Clans. The fruit of 'Project Omega,' a Free Worlds League Military project in 3052 to create an assault 'Mech capable of facing Clan OmniMechs based off scant reports of Clan fighting tactics and weapons and refit kits requested by the Federated Commonwealth and Draconis Combine, the first Albatross was delivered after only five months of design and testing with mass production being authorized by the League High Command a mere ten months after it was commissioned. Built using a Hermes 380 XL engine to minimize weight and maximize free tonnage for its weapon loadout, the Albatross also uses a variety of weapons in an attempt to make the 'Mech effective at any range. The 'Mech has average mobility with a maximum speed of 64.8 km/h and also makes use of double heat sinks for their greater heat dissipation capability. The Albatross carries thirteen tons of Starslab/3 armor. Finally, the 'Mech is also equipped with CASE to protect against ammunition explosions. The Albatross does not specialize in one role and is more of a workhorse design than anything else. The 4Ur is more a direct upgrade of the 3U than a refit of the 4U. The armor is maxed out with 16.5 tons of Ferro-Fibrous. The Light Gauss Rifle of the 4U is replaced with a familiar LBX-10 like the original 3U. A standard LRM-15 and SRM-6 fill out the right torso. A pair of ER Medium Lasers replace the standard lasers and sit atop the traditional Large Pulse Laser. A Light PPC sits in the left torso mount. When the 5U debuted to lack luster reception the AMSC fell back on the dependable performance of the 4Ur.

YangsThoughts: The only reason this thing ever got a bad rap was that some of the later versions used a composite internal structure which made it fragile if the armor was breached. The earlier versions are pefectly solid mechs, though they're a bit too generalist to really reach maximum effectiveness. Another dependable rework, which of these versions you like really does depend on preference of the pilot and what grade of weaponry you have available.
